documents.title: Bodyweight and pull-up bar only
documents.content: If equipment is limited to bodyweight and a pull-up bar, recommendations should stay within pull-up variations, rows under a sturdy setup, push-ups, split squats, lunges, hinges without a barbell, planks and tempo-based progression. Do not prescribe barbell lifts.

documents.title: Beginner general plan with dumbbells and pull-up bar
documents.content: For a beginner with dumbbells and a pull-up bar, use simple full-body sessions 2-3 times per week. Prioritize goblet squat, dumbbell bench press or floor press, dumbbell row, Romanian deadlift with light weight, overhead press, and assisted pull-up or inverted row. Start with moderate volume and leave 2-3 reps in reserve.

documents.title: Knee pain and no deep squats
documents.content: With knee pain or a restriction against deep squats, avoid recommending deep squat patterns. Prefer box squat to pain-free depth, split squat with reduced range, hip hinge patterns, glute bridge, Romanian deadlift, step-up with conservative depth and leg work that does not aggravate symptoms.
